title: Complete JWT auth redesign with RBAC hierarchy and user schema migration
description: Complete authentication system redesign with: (1) JWT-based auth replacing session store for API routes via withAuth, (2) Migrate Users collection schema to add permissions array and lastLogin timestamp fields with a migration script, (3) Implement hierarchical RBAC where admin > editor > viewer role inheritance is enforced in checkRole, (4) Ensure all protected API routes use JWT+RBAC middleware, (5) Add integration tests for full auth flow and migration script validation. Current gaps: Users collection missing permissions and lastLogin fields; checkRole lacks hierarchy support; auth-middleware.ts still uses SessionStore; no migration script for schema changes.
existing_patterns: src/auth/auth-service.ts - AuthService using Payload with JWT tokens (login, refresh, verify, logout)
src/auth/jwt-service.ts - JwtService with access/refresh token pairs and token rotation via generation field
src/auth/_auth.ts - checkRole function for RBAC (currently exact-match only, needs hierarchy)
src/auth/withAuth.ts - Higher-order function wrapping route handlers with JWT validation and RBAC
src/collections/Users.ts - Payload collection with role field (admin/editor/viewer) and token fields
src/middleware/role-guard.ts - requireRole guard function for route protection
vi.fn() mocks with mockResolvedValue/mockRejectedValue for Payload SDK stubs
questions: Should the SessionStore be completely removed or kept for backwards compatibility with other parts of the system?
What specific permissions should be included in the permissions array for each role (admin, editor, viewer)?
Should the migration script be a Payload migration (payload.config.ts hooks) or a standalone script?
